verdict: The locksmith occupation has a moderate AI displacement risk, with 36% of tasks potentially automatable.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Locksmiths perform tasks like key duplication, lock installation, and repair, which are partially automated by computer-aided design (CAD) software and 3D printing.

reasoning_steps.title: AI Capability Gap
reasoning_steps.body: Current AI models can already generate complex locking mechanisms and perform some locksmithing tasks, such as decoding locks, with high accuracy.

evidence_ledger.contribution: Locksmiths perform tasks that require manual dexterity and problem-solving skills.

adjacent_careers.name: Automotive Technician
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both locksmiths and automotive technicians work with mechanical devices and require manual dexterity and problem-solving skills.

adjacent_careers.name: Electrician
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Locksmiths and electricians both install and repair electrical systems, although locksmiths focus on locks and security devices.

recent_developments.headline: New Lock-Picking Tool Developed
recent_developments.impact: This development may increase the potential for AI-powered lock-picking tools in the future.

recent_developments.headline: Hands-on trades remain among the least automated
recent_developments.impact: Physical, in-situ work captures very little AI use today; trades have the lowest exposure of any major occupation group.

quotes.quote: We do not have enough plumbers, electricians, or HVAC techs. AI cannot fix a leaking pipe. The shortage in the trades is only going to widen.
quotes.speaker: Mike Rowe
quotes.title: Host of Dirty Jobs; founder, mikeroweWORKS Foundation

quotes.quote: The tasks most exposed to large language models are not physical. Plumbers, electricians, and construction workers have the lowest exposure of any major occupation group.
quotes.speaker: Tyna Eloundou and Pamela Mishkin
quotes.title: Researchers, OpenAI
quotes.source: GPTs are GPTs: An Early Look at the Labor Market Impact Potential of LLMs

quotes.quote: The world is short on skilled tradespeople and we have spent two generations telling kids the only path is a four-year degree. AI is now coming for the desk jobs first.
quotes.speaker: Mark Cuban
quotes.title: Investor; co-founder, Cost Plus Drugs
